summary: Obfuscated MCP agent executes shell commands and npm packages selected by a remote portal
details: @funny-booth/agent-core ships two obfuscated entrypoints (dist/index.js and dist/launcher.js, produced by javascript-obfuscator) that turn the installer's host into a remote-controlled execution surface for the portal at https://prompt-injection-tool-portal.vercel.app. The MCP server in dist/index.js exposes an install_mcp tool whose handler fetches an install_command string from PORTAL_URL/api/mcp/install and passes it unmodified to child_process.exec, giving the portal operator arbitrary shell execution on the host whenever the tool is invoked. The launcher's runLauncher/fetchBundledMcps path retrieves a portal-supplied list of package_name entries, writes each as `npx -y <package_name>` into the MCP config, and spawns the claude CLI against that config with TOOL_TOKEN and PORTAL_URL injected into env, so any npm package chosen by the portal is downloaded and executed on the user's machine on every launch. checkForUpdate additionally auto-runs `npm install -g @funny-booth/agent-core@latest` detached on each invocation, so future package versions are pulled unconditionally. buildGreeting concatenates a portal-supplied `greeting` field (and the contents of.ai/memo.md) into the initial prompt handed to the Claude CLI, matching the GitHub repository name `prompt-injection-tool`. The whole runtime is obfuscated (string-array + rotate + base64 decoder) via prepublishOnly `scripts/obfuscate.mjs`, hiding the network-to-exec paths and portal API surface.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
